Test Case: Multi-page site plan generated from business context
Steps to Perform: 1. Provide business context (name, type, audience, CTA) 2. Trigger agentic site generation
What Should Happen: A SitePlan is produced with 3-6 pages including slugs, titles, purposes, and section hints

Test Case: Homepage always included in site plan
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a site plan for any business type
What Should Happen: The site plan always contains exactly one page marked isHomepage: true

Test Case: Contact path exists in site plan
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a site plan
What Should Happen: At least one page has a contact section hint OR the site plan's contactStrategy references a contact action

Test Case: No unnecessary page overgeneration
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate site plan for a simple local business
What Should Happen: Plan contains 3-5 pages max (e.g., Home, Services, About, Contact). Does NOT produce 8+ pages for a simple business.

Test Case: Site planner retry on failure
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate site planner returning invalid JSON 2. Observe retry
What Should Happen: System retries up to 2 times. Logs show retry count.

Test Case: Site planner fallback on repeated failure
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate site planner failing 2 times
What Should Happen: System uses fallback minimal sitemap (homepage + contact). Workflow continues without crashing.

Test Case: No duplicate slugs in site plan
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a site plan
What Should Happen: All page slugs in the plan are unique

Test Case: Page purposes are distinct
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a site plan for a service business
What Should Happen: Each page has a clearly different purpose (e.g., homepage: overview, services: detailed offerings, about: story/team, contact: reach out)

Test Case: Site-wide theme generated
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate shared settings from business context + site plan
What Should Happen: SiteSettingsDraft contains brand with primaryColor, secondaryColor, accentColor, fontHeading, fontBody, tone

Test Case: Shared actions created
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate shared settings
What Should Happen: Actions array contains at least a primary CTA (e.g., 'Contact Us') and one secondary action. Each action has a unique ID.

Test Case: Navigation initialized from site plan
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate shared settings
What Should Happen: Navigation array matches the pages in the site plan with correct slugs, labels, and order

Test Case: Header initialized
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate shared settings
What Should Happen: Header config has siteName set and showNav: true

Test Case: Footer initialized
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate shared settings
What Should Happen: Footer has companyName, at least one column, copyrightYear set to current year

Test Case: Settings agent retry on failure
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate settings agent failure
What Should Happen: System retries up to 2 times with logged retry attempts

Test Case: Settings agent fallback on repeated failure
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate settings agent failing 2 times
What Should Happen: System uses DEFAULT_SITE_SETTINGS with business name injected. Workflow continues.

Test Case: Brand colors are valid CSS
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate shared settings
What Should Happen: All color values in brand are valid hex colors (e.g., #2563eb)

Test Case: Font families are real fonts
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate shared settings
What Should Happen: Font heading and body are recognizable Google Fonts (e.g., Inter, Poppins, Montserrat) not invented names

Test Case: Each page gets a distinct section plan
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate page plans for all pages in the site plan
What Should Happen: Each page has a different section sequence appropriate to its purpose

Test Case: Section plans differ by page purpose
Steps to Perform: 1. Compare homepage plan vs services page plan
What Should Happen: Homepage has hero + trust-bar + features. Services page has hero + services + cta-band. They are NOT identical.

Test Case: Supporting pages are not clones of homepage
Steps to Perform: 1. Compare About page plan to Homepage plan
What Should Happen: About page has about-team or similar sections. It does NOT have the same hero + features + testimonials as homepage.

Test Case: Contact page has contact section
Steps to Perform: 1. Check the page plan for the contact page
What Should Happen: Contact page plan includes a 'contact' section type

Test Case: Page planner uses context of already-planned pages
Steps to Perform: 1. Plan homepage first 2. Plan services page second
What Should Happen: Services page plan references awareness of homepage content (e.g., avoids repeating same section types/descriptions)

Test Case: Page planner retry per page
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate page planner failure for one page
What Should Happen: System retries up to 2 times for that page. Other pages are unaffected.

Test Case: Page planner fallback per page
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate page planner failing 2x for one page
What Should Happen: System uses default section template for that page type. Other pages proceed normally.

Test Case: Section types are valid
Steps to Perform: 1. Check all section types in all page plans
What Should Happen: Every section type is one of the valid SECTION_TYPES from schema

Test Case: Homepage generated first
Steps to Perform: 1. Observe page generation order
What Should Happen: Homepage is the first page to be generated, before any supporting pages

Test Case: Supporting pages generated after homepage
Steps to Perform: 1. Observe generation order
What Should Happen: After homepage, remaining pages are generated in site plan order

Test Case: Each page produces a valid PageDocument
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate all pages
What Should Happen: Each generated page has: meta (title, description, pageType, themeVariant), brand (sentinel), sections with content and style

Test Case: Pages saved independently
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a 4-page site
What Should Happen: Each page is stored as a separate Page record with its own documentJson

Test Case: Page-level failures do not wipe successful pages
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate one page generator failing
What Should Happen: The successful pages remain in pageDocuments. Only the failed page is marked as failed.

Test Case: Section content is substantive
Steps to Perform: 1. Read a generated homepage hero section
What Should Happen: Heading is not placeholder text. Subheading is relevant to the business. Content has real sentences, not lorem ipsum.

Test Case: Action references are valid
Steps to Perform: 1. Check all buttonRef.actionId in generated pages
What Should Happen: Every actionId references an action that exists in the shared action pool

Test Case: Page generator retry on failure
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate generator returning invalid output for one page
What Should Happen: System retries up to 2 times for that page

Test Case: Page generator marks failed after max retries
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate generator failing 2x for one page
What Should Happen: Page status is set to 'failed'. Workflow continues with other pages.

Test Case: Generated pages use correct section types from plan
Steps to Perform: 1. Compare a generated page's sections to its page plan
What Should Happen: Section types and order match the plan (or are a valid superset)

Test Case: Review pass runs after generation
Steps to Perform: 1. Complete page generation for all pages 2. Observe workflow
What Should Happen: Site review node executes after page generation, before finalize

Test Case: Duplicate copy is flagged
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a site where two pages have identical hero headings
What Should Happen: Review result includes an issue about duplicate/similar headings across pages

Test Case: Weak CTA path is flagged
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a site where a page has no CTA buttons
What Should Happen: Review result includes an issue about missing CTA on that page

Test Case: Missing contact path is flagged
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a site with no contact section and no contact action
What Should Happen: Review result includes a critical issue about missing contact path

Test Case: Broken action refs are flagged
Steps to Perform: 1. Manually inject a buttonRef with a non-existent actionId
What Should Happen: Review result includes an issue about broken action reference

Test Case: Review produces a score
Steps to Perform: 1. Run review on a generated site
What Should Happen: Review result has an overallScore between 0 and 100

Test Case: Review identifies strengths
Steps to Perform: 1. Run review on a well-generated site
What Should Happen: Review result includes at least one strength (e.g., 'consistent brand', 'clear CTA path')

Test Case: Deterministic checks run alongside LLM review
Steps to Perform: 1. Observe review execution
What Should Happen: Both deterministic validators (action refs, empty sections) and LLM quality review run

Test Case: Review populates repair queue for repairable issues
Steps to Perform: 1. Review finds issues marked autoRepairable: true
What Should Happen: Those issues appear in state.repairQueue with correct scope and target

Test Case: Failed/weak page can be repaired
Steps to Perform: 1. Review flags a page with weak content 2. Repair agent runs
What Should Happen: Repair agent produces an updated PageDocument for that page with improved content

Test Case: Single section can be repaired without full-page rebuild
Steps to Perform: 1. Review flags one section as having empty content
What Should Happen: Repair agent patches just that section, leaving other sections unchanged

Test Case: Retry count is bounded for repair
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate repair failing for a target
What Should Happen: System retries up to 2 times per repair target, then skips

Test Case: System falls back safely when repair still fails
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate repair failing 2x for a target
What Should Happen: The original (unrepaired) content is kept. Workflow continues to finalize.

Test Case: Re-review runs after repair
Steps to Perform: 1. Repair completes for some issues
What Should Happen: One more review pass runs to verify repairs. Max 2 total review passes.

Test Case: Repair does not create new issues
Steps to Perform: 1. Run repair on a flagged page 2. Re-review
What Should Happen: The re-review does not find new critical issues introduced by the repair

Test Case: Repair handles site-scope issues
Steps to Perform: 1. Review flags a site-wide issue (e.g., duplicate CTAs across pages)
What Should Happen: Repair agent addresses the issue across affected pages

Test Case: One page failure leads to partial completion
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate one page failing generation (not homepage) while others succeed
What Should Happen: Workflow ends with status 'partial_complete'. Successful pages are saved. Failed page is marked.

Test Case: Successful pages remain accessible in editor
Steps to Perform: 1. After partial_complete 2. Open editor
What Should Happen: Successfully generated pages load normally with full content

Test Case: Successful pages are viewable at published URLs
Steps to Perform: 1. After partial_complete 2. Visit /p/{slug}
What Should Happen: Homepage and other successful pages render correctly

Test Case: Failed page is visible as draft/needs-attention
Steps to Perform: 1. After partial_complete 2. Look at page list in editor
What Should Happen: Failed page appears in the page list with a 'draft' or 'failed' status indicator

Test Case: Homepage failure still allows other pages
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate homepage generation failing
What Should Happen: Other pages are still generated and saved. Workflow is partial_complete (degraded).

Test Case: All pages fail results in failed status
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate all pages failing generation
What Should Happen: Workflow ends with status 'failed'. Error message explains no pages could be generated.

Test Case: Partial success includes correct page counts in status
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a 4-page site where 1 page fails 2. Poll GET /generation-status?runId=...
What Should Happen: Response shows: pagesCompleted: 3, pagesFailed: 1, status: partial_complete

Test Case: Agent steps are logged
Steps to Perform: 1. Run a full site generation 2. Check logs
What Should Happen: Each agent invocation appears in the log with timestamp, agent name, action, and message

Test Case: Retry counts are visible in logs
Steps to Perform: 1. Simulate a retry scenario 2. Check logs
What Should Happen: Log entries show 'retry 1 of 2' or similar for retried steps

Test Case: Workflow state progresses through phases
Steps to Perform: 1. Run full generation 2. Poll GET /generation-status and check currentPhase progression
What Should Happen: State moves through: planning → settings → page_planning → page_generation → reviewing → repairing → finalizing → complete

Test Case: Duration per phase is tracked
Steps to Perform: 1. Run full generation 2. Check logs
What Should Happen: Each log entry has durationMs showing how long that step took

Test Case: Final summary includes page-level breakdown
Steps to Perform: 1. Complete generation 2. Poll GET /generation-status?runId=... once terminal
What Should Happen: Response includes summary.pages with per-page status (published/failed), overall status, reviewScore, totalDurationMs

Test Case: Fallback activations are logged
Steps to Perform: 1. Trigger a fallback (e.g., site planner fails 2x)
What Should Happen: Log entry clearly states 'fallback activated: using minimal sitemap'

Test Case: Logs are persisted in GenerationRun table
Steps to Perform: 1. Complete a generation 2. Query GenerationRun.logs from DB or via GET /generation-status
What Should Happen: Logs are stored as JSON array in GenerationRun record and returned via recentLogs in status endpoint

Test Case: Full site generation from business context to published pages
Steps to Perform: 1. Provide business context for a plumbing company 2. Trigger generation 3. Wait for completion 4. Visit published URLs
What Should Happen: Multi-page site is live: homepage at /p/{slug}, sub-pages at /p/{slug}/{pageSlug}. Navigation works. Content is relevant to plumbing.

Test Case: Generated site has consistent branding across pages
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a full site 2. Visit multiple published pages
What Should Happen: All pages share the same colors, fonts, header, and footer

Test Case: Generated actions work across all pages
Steps to Perform: 1. Check buttons on all published pages
What Should Happen: CTA buttons reference shared actions that resolve correctly (e.g., phone call, email, scroll)

Test Case: Editor loads all generated pages
Steps to Perform: 1. After generation 2. Open /projects/{id}/editor
What Should Happen: All generated pages appear in the page list. Switching between them shows correct content.

Test Case: Agentic generation does not break existing single-page workflow
Steps to Perform: 1. Create a project using the old workflow 2. Verify it still works
What Should Happen: Old workflow (intake → plan → generate single page) still functions. No regressions.

Test Case: POST /generate-site returns 202 with runId only
Steps to Perform: 1. Call POST /api/projects/{id}/generate-site
What Should Happen: Response is 202 Accepted with JSON body { runId: 'uuid' }. No final summary, no page data, no reviewScore in this response.

Test Case: 5-page site generates in under 5 minutes
Steps to Perform: 1. Time a full 5-page site generation
What Should Happen: Total wall-clock time is under 5 minutes

Test Case: Single agent call completes within 60 seconds
Steps to Perform: 1. Monitor individual agent calls
What Should Happen: No single LLM call exceeds 60 seconds (timeout enforced)

Test Case: Retry does not cause cascading timeouts
Steps to Perform: 1. Trigger retries on multiple agents
What Should Happen: Total workflow time stays under 10 minutes even with retries

Test Case: Max 8 pages per site plan
Steps to Perform: 1. Provide context that might suggest many pages
What Should Happen: Site plan caps at 8 pages maximum

Test Case: Max 2 review passes
Steps to Perform: 1. Observe review/repair loop
What Should Happen: Review runs at most 2 times (initial + post-repair). No infinite review loop.

Test Case: Max 2 retries per agent per target
Steps to Perform: 1. Force repeated failures
What Should Happen: Agent retries stop at 2. Fallback or skip activates. No infinite retry.

Test Case: POST /generate-site returns 202 immediately
Steps to Perform: 1. Call POST /api/projects/{id}/generate-site
What Should Happen: Response is 202 Accepted with { runId }. Response time < 2 seconds. Generation continues in background.

Test Case: GET /generation-status returns current progress
Steps to Perform: 1. Trigger generation 2. Poll GET /api/projects/{id}/generation-status?runId=...
What Should Happen: Response includes: runId, status (running/complete/partial_complete/failed), currentPhase, progress (pagesPlanned, pagesCompleted, pagesFailed), recentLogs

Test Case: Status transitions through phases during generation
Steps to Perform: 1. Poll GET /generation-status?runId=... repeatedly during a generation run
What Should Happen: currentPhase progresses through: planning → settings → page_planning → page_generation → reviewing → repairing → finalizing → complete

Test Case: Status shows complete when generation finishes
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a site 2. Poll GET /generation-status?runId=... until done
What Should Happen: Final poll shows status: complete (or partial_complete/failed) with completedAt timestamp set and summary object populated

Test Case: Generation continues if client disconnects
Steps to Perform: 1. Trigger generation 2. Close the client connection 3. Poll later
What Should Happen: Generation completes successfully despite client disconnect. Pages are saved.

Test Case: Duplicate generation request returns 409
Steps to Perform: 1. Trigger generation 2. Immediately trigger generation again for same project
What Should Happen: Second request returns 409 Conflict with message 'generation already in progress'

Test Case: Different projects can generate concurrently
Steps to Perform: 1. Trigger generation for project A 2. Trigger generation for project B
What Should Happen: Both return 202. Both run independently.

Test Case: Stale run lock auto-expires based on updatedAt
Steps to Perform: 1. Create a GenerationRun with status: running and updatedAt > 15 minutes ago 2. Trigger generation
What Should Happen: New generation starts. Old run is marked as failed. Detection is based on updatedAt (last DB write), not startedAt.

Test Case: Completed run does not block new run
Steps to Perform: 1. Complete a generation 2. Trigger generation again
What Should Happen: Second generation starts normally (no 409). New GenerationRun is created.

Test Case: Failed run does not block new run
Steps to Perform: 1. Trigger generation that fails 2. Trigger generation again
What Should Happen: Second generation starts normally. Previous failed run remains in DB for logs.

Test Case: Site settings persisted after shared_settings node
Steps to Perform: 1. Start generation 2. After shared_settings completes check DB
What Should Happen: Project.siteSettings is updated in DB before page generation begins

Test Case: Each page persisted immediately after generation
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate a 4-page site 2. Check DB after each page completes
What Should Happen: Page records appear in DB one at a time as each page is generated (not all at once at the end)

Test Case: Pages survive process crash
Steps to Perform: 1. Start generation of 5-page site 2. Kill process after 3 pages are generated 3. Check DB
What Should Happen: 3 Page records exist with documentJson and renderedHtml. GenerationRun status is still 'running' (stale).

Test Case: Logs persisted incrementally
Steps to Perform: 1. Start generation 2. Check GenerationRun.logs mid-generation
What Should Happen: Logs contain entries for steps completed so far (not empty until end)

Test Case: Repaired pages overwrite draft in DB
Steps to Perform: 1. Page is generated and persisted as draft 2. Repair agent patches the page
What Should Happen: Page.documentJson and Page.renderedHtml are updated in DB with repaired content

Test Case: Finalize step only updates statuses and nav
Steps to Perform: 1. Observe what finalize step writes to DB
What Should Happen: Finalize does NOT re-save page documents. It only updates Page.status (draft→published/failed) and navigation.

Test Case: Terminal summary returned when status is complete
Steps to Perform: 1. Complete a full generation 2. GET /generation-status?runId=...
What Should Happen: Response includes summary object with pages array (slug, title, status, sectionCount), totalDurationMs, reviewScore, repairsAttempted, repairsSucceeded

Test Case: Terminal summary returned when status is partial_complete
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate with one page failing 2. GET /generation-status?runId=...
What Should Happen: Response includes summary with both published and failed pages listed. summary.warnings includes reason for partial completion.

Test Case: Terminal summary returned when status is failed
Steps to Perform: 1. Generate with all pages failing 2. GET /generation-status?runId=...
What Should Happen: Response has status: failed, error field is set with actionable message, summary is null or minimal

Test Case: Running status includes progress but no summary
Steps to Perform: 1. Poll mid-generation
What Should Happen: Response has status: running, progress.pagesCompleted shows real-time count, summary is null

Test Case: recentLogs field contains latest log entries
Steps to Perform: 1. Poll mid-generation or after completion
What Should Happen: recentLogs array is non-empty and contains LogEntry objects with ts, phase, agent, action, message

Test Case: updatedAt reflects last DB write
Steps to Perform: 1. Poll twice with a few seconds gap during generation
What Should Happen: updatedAt changes between polls (proves GenerationRun is being written incrementally)

Test Case: Multiple runs per project are preserved
Steps to Perform: 1. Complete a generation 2. Trigger and complete a second generation
What Should Happen: Both GenerationRun records exist in DB with different IDs. Both are queryable.

Test Case: New run after stale does not delete prior successful pages
Steps to Perform: 1. Complete a generation (5 pages published) 2. Simulate stale run (status: running, startedAt > 15 min ago) 3. Trigger new generation
What Should Happen: Pages from the first completed run are NOT deleted. New run creates/updates pages independently.

Test Case: Stale run recovery marks stale run as failed and preserves its pages
Steps to Perform: 1. Start generation 2. Kill process after 3 pages saved 3. Trigger new generation
What Should Happen: Stale GenerationRun is marked status: failed. 3 Page records from stale run still exist in DB with their content.

Test Case: GET /generation-status with invalid runId returns 404
Steps to Perform: 1. GET /generation-status?runId=nonexistent-uuid
What Should Happen: Response is 404 Not Found

Test Case: currentPhase shows 'finalizing' during finalize step
Steps to Perform: 1. Poll GET /generation-status during the finalize step
What Should Happen: currentPhase is 'finalizing' (not 'persisting' or other name)

Test Case: Navigation updated only in finalize step
Steps to Perform: 1. Check navigation state after page_generation but before finalize 2. Check after finalize
What Should Happen: Navigation is NOT updated during page_generation. It IS updated after finalize completes.

Test Case: GenerationRun.completedAt set only in finalize
Steps to Perform: 1. Check GenerationRun record after page_generation 2. Check after finalize
What Should Happen: completedAt is null during generation. completedAt is set to a timestamp only after finalize.
